cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out which of the options A, B, C, or D is correct based on the passage provided. Let me go through the passage again carefully and check each option. First, the passage talks about the collapse of the Sacramento River salmon run. The key points mentioned are that last fall, about 90,000 adult salmon returned, which is the second lowest number on record. That's for option A. So A says "Last fall, there was the second lowest number of grown salmon returning on record." The passage does say "second lowest number on record," so A seems to be correct. But let me check the other options to be sure. Option B: "The salmon migrates to the ocean when they grow up." The passage states, "wild salmon are born in streams and rivers, migrate to the ocean when they're juveniles..." So "juveniles" means young, not when they grow up. So B is incorrect because it's when they're juveniles, not when they grow up. Option C: "The farm-raised salmon is not as delicious and nutrients as the wild salmon." The passage mentions that there will be farm-raised salmon available but doesn't compare their taste or nutrition to wild salmon. So C isn't stated in the text. The passage just says that Chinook (wild) is prized by trollers, etc., and that farm-raised might be more expensive, but it doesn't say anything about the quality of farm-raised being worse. So C is not a correct statement based on the passage. Option D: "Salmon fishing off the coasts of California and Oregon is strictly forbidden."
